mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This reflects an accommodative monetary policy stance.
evidence_refs: fact:low interest rates generally support equity valuations
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes a slowdown in consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances following the trigger, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a low federal funds rate indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy, driving corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) when both conditions are met, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a period of high inflation (implied by CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary pressures, where supply constraints lead to lower corporate output and margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:stagflation is detrimental to equity markets
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 35% of triggered instances during periods where CPIAUCSL is above 3% annualized, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts indicates a slowdown in the construction sector and broader economic demand, which typically translates to weaker corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls below 1000 (thousands of units)
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods following the trigger, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust non-farm payroll employment growth indicates a strong labor market and healthy consumer base, supporting corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 45% of instances when the trigger is met, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and increases input costs for businesses, potentially leading to lower profit margins and negative S&P 500 forward returns as central banks may tighten policy over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation can be detrimental to equity valuations
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following the trigger, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth signifies a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a favorable environment for corporate earnings and typically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary driver of corporate profits
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances when the trigger is met, this hypothesis is rejected.
